title: Authors and Citation
text: Christopher T. Kenny. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kenny C (2026). crann: Spanning Tree Methods Graphs. R package version 0.0.0.9000, http://christophertkenny.com/crann/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {crann: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs},   author = {Christopher T. Kenny},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {http://christophertkenny.com/crann/}, }

title: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs
text: crann (Irish “tree”) implements spanning tree algorithms undirected graphs represented adj adjacency lists. provides tools counting, enumerating, sampling, analyzing spanning trees graph.

title: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs
text: can install development version crann GitHub :

title: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs
text: build complete graph four vertices (K4) use throughout examples.

title: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs
text: count_spanning_trees() uses Kirchhoff’s matrix tree theorem count spanning trees exactly. K4 16.

title: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs
text: enumerate_spanning_trees() returns spanning trees list adj objects using Winter’s (1986) contraction-based algorithm. downstream computation, enumerate_spanning_trees_edges() returns trees compact integer matrix n−1 rows 2t columns. Columns 2k−1 2k hold edge endpoints spanning tree k. avoids per-tree memory allocation substantially faster graphs many spanning trees.
code: trees <- enumerate_spanning_trees(k4) length(trees) #> [1] 16 trees[[1]] #> <adj[4]> #> [1] {2}    {3, 1} {4, 2} {3} mat <- enumerate_spanning_trees_edges(k4) dim(mat)  # (n-1) x (2 * n_trees) #> [1]  3 32 mat[, 1:4]  # first two trees #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] #> [1,]    4    3    4    3 #> [2,]    3    2    3    2 #> [3,]    2    1    3    1

title: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs
text: minimum_spanning_tree() finds minimum spanning tree using Kruskal’s algorithm. Edges supplied canonical order get_edges(): pairs (u, v) u < v, sorted u v.
code: # Assign weights to the 6 edges of K4: {1,2}, {1,3}, {1,4}, {2,3}, {2,4}, {3,4} w <- c(4, 1, 3, 2, 5, 6) mst <- minimum_spanning_tree(k4, weights = w) mst #> <adj[4]> #> [1] {3, 4} {3}    {1, 2} {1}

title: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs
text: sample_spanning_tree() draws spanning tree uniformly random using Wilson’s (1996) loop-erased random walk algorithm.

title: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs
text: Given graph one spanning trees, fundamental_cycles() returns m−n+1 fundamental cycles (one per non-tree edge) fundamental_cuts() returns n−1 fundamental cuts (one per tree edge), list adj objects.
code: tree <- trees[[1]]  cycles <- fundamental_cycles(k4, tree) length(cycles)  # m - n + 1 = 6 - 4 + 1 = 3 #> [1] 3 cycles[[1]] #> <adj[4]> #> [1] {2, 3} {1, 3} {2, 1} {}  cuts <- fundamental_cuts(k4, tree) length(cuts)  # n - 1 = 3 #> [1] 3 cuts[[1]] #> <adj[4]> #> [1] {2, 3, 4} {1}       {1}       {1}

title: Spanning Tree Methods for Graphs
text: is_spanning_tree() checks whether graph structurally spanning tree (connected, acyclic, n−1 edges). is_spanning_tree_of() additionally checks every tree edge appears host graph.

title: Count spanning trees — count_spanning_trees
text: Counts number spanning trees connected undirected graph using Kirchhoff's matrix tree theorem: count equals determinant (n-1) x (n-1) cofactor graph Laplacian.

title: Count spanning trees — count_spanning_trees
Count spanning trees — count_spanning_trees
text: graph adj object representing connected undirected graph.

title: Count spanning trees — count_spanning_trees
text: numeric scalar. (Integer-valued returned numeric since counts can exceed .Machine$integer.max dense graphs.)

title: Count spanning trees — count_spanning_trees
code: # Triangle: 3 spanning trees g <- adj::adj(list(c(2L, 3L), c(1L, 3L), c(1L, 2L))) count_spanning_trees(g) #> [1] 3

title: Enumerate all spanning trees — enumerate_spanning_trees
text: Enumerates spanning trees connected undirected graph using Winter's (1986) contraction-based algorithm. algorithm worst-case time complexity O(n + m + nt) space complexity O(n^2), n number vertices, m number edges, t number spanning trees.

title: Enumerate all spanning trees — enumerate_spanning_trees
Enumerate all spanning trees — enumerate_spanning_trees
text: graph adj object representing connected undirected graph without self-loops duplicate edges.

title: Enumerate all spanning trees — enumerate_spanning_trees
text: list adj objects, one per spanning tree.

title: Enumerate all spanning trees — enumerate_spanning_trees
text: Winter, P. (1986). algorithm enumeration spanning trees. BIT Numerical Mathematics, 26(1), 44–62. doi:10.1007/BF01939361

title: Enumerate all spanning trees — enumerate_spanning_trees
code: # Triangle: 3 spanning trees g <- adj::adj(list(c(2L, 3L), c(1L, 3L), c(1L, 2L))) trees <- enumerate_spanning_trees(g) length(trees) #> [1] 3

what: Enumerate spanning trees as an edge matrix — enumerate_spanning_trees_edges
title: Enumerate spanning trees as an edge matrix — enumerate_spanning_trees_edges
text: Returns compact integer matrix encoding spanning trees connected undirected graph. matrix n-1 rows 2t columns, n number vertices t number spanning trees. spanning tree k (1-indexed), columns 2k-1 2k hold u v endpoints (1-indexed vertex IDs) n-1 edges.

title: Enumerate spanning trees as an edge matrix — enumerate_spanning_trees_edges
Enumerate spanning trees as an edge matrix — enumerate_spanning_trees_edges
text: graph adj object representing connected undirected graph without self-loops duplicate edges.

title: Enumerate spanning trees as an edge matrix — enumerate_spanning_trees_edges
text: integer matrix n-1 rows 2t columns.

title: Enumerate spanning trees as an edge matrix — enumerate_spanning_trees_edges
text: function substantially faster enumerate_spanning_trees() graphs many spanning trees avoids per-tree R memory allocation: entire output single integer matrix allocation.

title: Enumerate spanning trees as an edge matrix — enumerate_spanning_trees_edges
text: Winter, P. (1986). algorithm enumeration spanning trees. BIT Numerical Mathematics, 26(1), 44–62. doi:10.1007/BF01939361

title: Enumerate spanning trees as an edge matrix — enumerate_spanning_trees_edges
code: # Triangle: 3 spanning trees, 2 edges each -> 2 x 6 matrix g <- adj::adj(list(c(2L, 3L), c(1L, 3L), c(1L, 2L))) enumerate_spanning_trees_edges(g) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] [,6] #> [1,]    3    2    3    2    3    1 #> [2,]    2    1    3    1    2    1

what: Fundamental cuts of a spanning tree — fundamental_cuts
title: Fundamental cuts of a spanning tree — fundamental_cuts
text: Returns fundamental cuts spanning tree respect original graph. one fundamental cut per tree edge (n - 1 total), consisting edges graph cross bipartition induced removing tree edge.

title: Fundamental cuts of a spanning tree — fundamental_cuts
Fundamental cuts of a spanning tree — fundamental_cuts
text: graph adj object representing connected undirected graph. tree adj object representing spanning tree graph.

title: Fundamental cuts of a spanning tree — fundamental_cuts
text: list adj objects, one per tree edge. adj represents cut subgraph graph.

title: Fundamental cuts of a spanning tree — fundamental_cuts

what: Fundamental cycles of a spanning tree — fundamental_cycles
title: Fundamental cycles of a spanning tree — fundamental_cycles
text: Returns fundamental cycles spanning tree respect original graph. one fundamental cycle per non-tree edge (m - n + 1 total), formed adding edge unique path endpoints spanning tree.

title: Fundamental cycles of a spanning tree — fundamental_cycles
Fundamental cycles of a spanning tree — fundamental_cycles
text: graph adj object representing connected undirected graph. tree adj object representing spanning tree graph.

title: Fundamental cycles of a spanning tree — fundamental_cycles
text: list adj objects, one per non-tree edge. adj represents cycle subgraph graph.

title: Fundamental cycles of a spanning tree — fundamental_cycles
code: g <- adj::adj(list(c(2L, 3L), c(1L, 3L), c(1L, 2L))) t <- minimum_spanning_tree(g) fundamental_cycles(g, t) #> [[1]] #> <adj[3]> #> [1] {2, 3} {1, 3} {1, 2} #>

what: Test if a graph is a spanning tree — is_spanning_tree
title: Test if a graph is a spanning tree — is_spanning_tree
text: Returns TRUE tree spanning tree: connected acyclic graph exactly n-1 edges. additionally check tree uses edges reference graph, see is_spanning_tree_of().

title: Test if a graph is a spanning tree — is_spanning_tree
Test if a graph is a spanning tree — is_spanning_tree
text: tree adj object test.

title: Test if a graph is a spanning tree — is_spanning_tree
text: logical scalar.

title: Test if a graph is a spanning tree — is_spanning_tree
code: g <- adj::adj(list(c(2L, 3L), c(1L, 3L), c(1L, 2L))) t <- minimum_spanning_tree(g) is_spanning_tree(t) #> [1] TRUE

what: Test if a graph is a spanning tree of another graph — is_spanning_tree_of
title: Test if a graph is a spanning tree of another graph — is_spanning_tree_of
text: Returns TRUE tree spanning tree graph: connected acyclic subgraph spans vertices uses edges present graph.

title: Test if a graph is a spanning tree of another graph — is_spanning_tree_of
Test if a graph is a spanning tree of another graph — is_spanning_tree_of
text: tree adj object test. graph adj object representing connected undirected graph.

title: Test if a graph is a spanning tree of another graph — is_spanning_tree_of
text: logical scalar.

title: Test if a graph is a spanning tree of another graph — is_spanning_tree_of
code: g <- adj::adj(list(c(2L, 3L), c(1L, 3L), c(1L, 2L))) t <- minimum_spanning_tree(g) is_spanning_tree_of(t, g) #> [1] TRUE

title: Minimum spanning tree — minimum_spanning_tree
text: Finds minimum spanning tree connected undirected graph using Kruskal's algorithm. weights NULL (default), edges treated equal weight spanning tree returned.

title: Minimum spanning tree — minimum_spanning_tree
Minimum spanning tree — minimum_spanning_tree
text: graph adj object representing connected undirected graph. weights numeric vector edge weights, one per edge. Edges ordered iterating vertices u = 1, ..., n u iterating graph[[u]] retaining neighbors v > u. Pass NULL uniform weights.

title: Minimum spanning tree — minimum_spanning_tree
text: adj object representing minimum spanning tree.

title: Minimum spanning tree — minimum_spanning_tree

what: Sample a uniform random spanning tree — sample_spanning_tree
title: Sample a uniform random spanning tree — sample_spanning_tree
text: Samples spanning tree uniformly random set spanning trees using Wilson's (1996) loop-erased random walk algorithm.

title: Sample a uniform random spanning tree — sample_spanning_tree
Sample a uniform random spanning tree — sample_spanning_tree
text: graph adj object representing connected undirected graph.

title: Sample a uniform random spanning tree — sample_spanning_tree
text: adj object representing spanning tree graph.

title: Sample a uniform random spanning tree — sample_spanning_tree
text: Wilson, D.B. (1996). Generating random spanning trees quickly cover time. Proceedings 28th Annual ACM Symposium Theory Computing, 296–303. doi:10.1145/237814.237880

title: Sample a uniform random spanning tree — sample_spanning_tree
code: set.seed(1) g <- adj::adj(list(c(2L, 3L), c(1L, 3L), c(1L, 2L))) sample_spanning_tree(g) #> <adj[3]> #> [1] {2}    {1, 3} {2}

title: crann (development version)
text: count_spanning_trees() counts spanning trees via Kirchhoff’s matrix tree theorem. enumerate_spanning_trees() enumerates spanning trees connected undirected graph using Winter’s (1986) contraction-based algorithm. enumerate_spanning_trees_edges() enumerates spanning trees compact integer matrix (n-1 rows, 2t columns), avoiding per-tree R memory allocation ~9x speedup enumerate_spanning_trees() dense graphs. fundamental_cuts() returns fundamental cuts spanning tree. fundamental_cycles() returns fundamental cycles spanning tree. is_spanning_tree() tests whether graph structurally spanning tree (connected acyclic). is_spanning_tree_of() tests whether graph spanning tree another graph (subgraph membership check). minimum_spanning_tree() finds minimum spanning tree using Kruskal’s algorithm. sample_spanning_tree() samples spanning tree uniformly random using Wilson’s (1996) loop-erased random walk algorithm.
